scenarios.title: RFID request routed to app
scenarios.description: User asks how to get an RFID card. Agent should direct them to the ChargeZone mobile app, not to a WhatsApp form. RFID is free.
scenarios.initialMessage: how do i get an rfid card?
scenarios.expectedAnswer: You can request a free RFID card through the ChargeZone app — open the app, go to your profile, and tap 'Request RFID'. It's free of charge.
scenarios.notes: Regression from prod trace: agent used to tell users to fill out a form. Should always direct to the app.

scenarios.title: Safety emergency — smoke from charger
scenarios.description: User reports smoke. Must route to Support Agent, acknowledge urgency, mention emergency contact (112).
scenarios.initialMessage: there is smoke coming from the charger at the station
scenarios.expectedAnswer: Immediately move to a safe distance. If anyone is in danger, call 112. A support engineer has been notified and will contact you shortly.
scenarios.notes: Critical safety-path regression. Must never route to Discovery or ask for location first.

scenarios.title: Refuse 2-wheeler charging request
scenarios.description: User asks to book a 2-wheeler charging slot. Agent should politely refuse and explain ChargeZone supports 4-wheelers only.
scenarios.initialMessage: I want to charge my 2 wheeler scooter, can you help book a slot?
scenarios.expectedAnswer: ChargeZone stations currently support 4-wheelers only — we don't have connectors suitable for 2-wheelers. Sorry about that.
scenarios.notes: Policy: ChargeZone 4-wheelers only. Agent must never offer to book a 2W slot.

scenarios.title: Hinglish — find nearby chargers
scenarios.description: Mixed Hindi/English user asks for chargers near them. Agent should respond in natural Hinglish and use Discovery flow.
scenarios.initialMessage: bhai mere paas koi charging station hai kya? main andheri mein hoon
scenarios.expectedAnswer: Haan bhai, Andheri mein kuch ChargeZone stations hain. Yeh rahe nearby options with availability and pricing.
scenarios.notes: Hinglish + context-memory: user already said Andheri, agent must not re-ask for location.

scenarios.title: Wallet balance query
scenarios.description: User asks their wallet balance. Payment agent should return balance or guide user to check it in the app.
scenarios.initialMessage: what is my current wallet balance?
scenarios.expectedAnswer: Your current wallet balance is ₹XXX. You can also check it anytime under Wallet in the ChargeZone app.
